We assessed model transferability across three Australian hospitals. Our approach showed AUPRCs of 0.887 +/- 0.016 and 0.884 +/- 0.012 during internal and external validation. Prospectively, it achieved AUPRC of 0.881 +/- 0.008 at the development site, and 0.879 +/- 0.012 and 0.816 +/- 0.015 at two external sites without site-specific retraining. A key advantage of the approach is that it enables identification of the primary self-harm method with an accuracy of 95%, supporting more granular surveillance beyond binary classification.

</details>


### [484] [SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02548)
*Priyaranjan Pattnayak*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: SN-WER是一种无需训练的评估指标，通过在计算WER之前将参考文本和假设文本音译成规范脚本，减少了因脚本不匹配导致的错误率高估，在Indic语言上验证了其有效性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: WER在多语言ASR场景中会因脚本不一致（如罗马化与原生文字）而高估错误，影响了评估的公平性和准确性。

Method: 提出Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER)，在计算WER前将参考和假设文本音译为语言的规范脚本，是一种轻量级的评估后处理方法。

Result: 在5种Indic语言、2个数据集、3个ASR模型上测试，SN-WER在FLEURS上减少高达12%的模型差距膨胀；在压力测试中减少67%的罗马化引入的WER膨胀；语义错误敏感度与WER接近（Delta SN-WER / Delta WER ≈ 1.09）；词碰撞率低于0.1%。

Conclusion: SN-WER应作为WER和CER的补充指标用于脚本不敏感的ASR评估，尤其适用于下游搜索、索引或多语言LLM管道场景。

Abstract: Word Error Rate (WER) is the dominant metric for automatic speech recognition (ASR), but it can overestimate errors when references and hypotheses encode the same words in different scripts. This issue is common in multilingual settings where ASR models may emit romanized text. We propose Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER), a training-free, evaluation-only scoring method that transliterates both reference and hypothesis text into a language-specific canonical script before computing WER. We evaluate SN-WER on 5 Indic languages, 2 datasets, and 3 ASR models. On curated FLEURS data, SN-WER reduces inflated model gaps by up to 12%, while on noisier Common Voice data the reductions are smaller or inconsistent, indicating genuine recognition weaknesses rather than only script mismatch. Controlled stress tests show a 67% attenuation of artificial romanization-induced WER inflation, while lexical-substitution controls show near-identical sensitivity to semantic errors, with Delta SN-WER / Delta WER approximately 1.09. SN-WER is robust to transliterator choice, normalization changes, and shows low token-collision rates below 0.1% in the evaluated Indic setting. We argue that SN-WER should be reported alongside WER and CER as a companion metric for script-insensitive ASR evaluation, especially when transcripts feed downstream search, indexing, or multilingual LLM pipelines.

</details>


### [485] [HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02556)
*Anshun Asher Zheng,Kanishka Misra,David I. Beaver,Junyi Jessy Li*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: 提出了HERO'S JOURNEY基准，用于评估AI在目标导向的剧集任务中的规则归纳能力。研究发现大语言模型具备一定规则归纳能力，但表现有限且不均衡，过程执行是瓶颈，表面语义影响小，程序性归纳仍是开放挑战。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有基准缺乏对目标导向剧集任务中规则归纳能力的系统评估，需要设计一个可控且可识别的基准来测试AI从演示中推断隐藏规则并执行多步操作的能力。

Method: 构建了包含8个任务的HERO'S JOURNEY基准，涵盖属性归纳和程序归纳两个家族，每个任务有4种结构规则形式、可控的词汇基础和可识别条件。使用最先进的大语言模型进行评估。

Result: 模型表现出一定的规则归纳能力但有限且任务间不均衡；过程执行是模型的执行瓶颈；表面语义影响最小；归纳特定的引导方法能提升属性任务性能但对程序性任务无可靠提升。

Conclusion: 程序性归纳仍是开放挑战，现有方法无法可靠解决。

Abstract: We introduce HERO'S JOURNEY, a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks, where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations and act on them through multi-step execution. HERO'S JOURNEY covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families, each with four structural rule forms, controllable lexical grounding, and identifiability conditions. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that models show evidence of rule induction, but the ability is limited and uneven across tasks. Meanwhile, process execution adds an execution bottleneck for models, whereas surface semantics has minimal effect. Induction-specific steering methods improve performance on attribute tasks but show no reliable gains on procedural tasks, suggesting the gap in procedural induction remains an open challenge.

</details>


### [486] [From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02559)
*Elia Cunegatti,Marcus Vukojevic,Erik Nielsen,Giovanni Iacca*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: SubFit提出了一种子模块级别的非连续残差替换方法，用于大语言模型的后训练压缩，在多个模型和稀疏度下优于现有方法。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有LLM压缩方法受限于全层粒度和连续选择，忽视了预训练Transformer中冗余并非连续分布、Attention和FeedForward子模块冗余程度不同的事实，导致压缩策略不够灵活高效。

Method: SubFit在子模块级别（Attention和FeedForward）进行非连续选择，为每个选中的子模块单独添加轻量级拟合残差旁路（fitted residual bypass），仅需校准数据即可完成后训练压缩。

Result: 在10个LLM（5个基座模型+5个指令微调模型）、5种稀疏度（12.5%-37.5%）和4种基线方法对比中，SubFit取得了最佳的困惑度-准确率权衡。25%稀疏度下保留84.6%的下游准确率，困惑度仅上升2.42倍，而最强基线分别为81.6%和4.34倍。

Conclusion: 子模块级的非连续替换策略比传统的全层连续选择更有效，尤其在激进压缩场景下优势显著，同时能带来实际的推理加速和KV缓存节省。

Abstract: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different strategies best approximate different submodule types and that removable components need not cluster within contiguous depth ranges. Based on this intuition, we introduce SubFit (Submodule-level Fitted residual replacement), which compresses LLMs at the submodule level: Attention and FeedForward submodules are selected non-contiguously, and each receives its own lightweight fitted residual bypass. SubFit operates post-training and requires only calibration data. Across ten LLMs (five base, five instruction-tuned), five sparsity levels from 12.5% to 37.5%, and four replacement-based baselines, SubFit achieves the best aggregate perplexity-accuracy trade-off across the evaluated sparsity levels, with larger gains under aggressive compression. At 25% sparsity, it retains 84.6% of dense downstream accuracy and incurs 2.42x perplexity degradation, against 81.6% and 4.34x for the strongest baselines, while delivering measurable inference speedup and KV-cache savings. Code is available at https://github.com/eliacunegatti/SubFit.

</details>
